title: AI-assisted-tracing instruction guide (RapiD, human-confirmed)
context: RapiD is an OSM editor that surfaces AI-detected feature suggestions for human mappers to accept, modify, or reject one at a time. It keeps AI assistance within OSM community norms: the human confirms every edit in a standard OSM editor. open-map-gaps can recommend RapiD as an efficiency tool for mappers, but only with clear guidance on the human-confirmation discipline required. This guide explains how to use RapiD (or similar AI-suggestion editors) within the project's guardrails and OSM community expectations.
objective: Author and commit a guide on using AI-assisted tracing editors (RapiD) for open-map-gaps packs, covering human-confirmation discipline, OSM community norms, and what AI suggestions must not be accepted without review.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide explains RapiD (and similar AI-suggestion editors) and when they are appropriate for open-map-gaps packs.
Guide covers the human-confirmation discipline: each AI suggestion must be individually reviewed and confirmed or rejected by a human before committing.
Guide explains what AI suggestions must not be accepted without careful review (e.g., building footprints in sensitive areas, any feature that could be people-level data).
Guide explicitly states that the AI-suggestion editor must not be used in a way that bypasses the do-no-harm gate or the imagery gate.
Guide is reviewed by the technical reviewer and the do-no-harm reviewer.
resources: OSM community norms on automated/AI editing — https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Automated_Edits_code_of_conduct
output: A committed guide on using AI-assisted tracing editors (RapiD) for open-map-gaps packs, covering human-confirmation discipline, OSM community norms, and guardrails against AI-suggestion misuse.
